title: wollastonia micrantha subsp. exigua
intro: Wollastonia micrantha subsp. exigua is a sub-species of the Wollastonia micrantha plant, a type of flowering plant native to the tropical regions of Asia, particularly in India and Southeast Asia. The plant is known for its medicinal properties, particularly its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can be used to treat various health issues. The health benefits of Wollastonia micrantha subsp. exigua include its ability to reduce inflammation, improve wound healing, and exhibit antimicrobial activity against various pathogens. The herbal preparations of this plant typically involve making extracts or infusions from the leaves and roots, which can be used to treat various health conditions, including skin infections and wounds.
